Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind / Pam Kowalski. Discovers: Pam's apartment complaint (CSAT 5/10), her formal final warning to David Chen (April 24) demanding a retention proposal by Friday and naming PathFinder Logistics as a competitor, her escalation citing Catalina's slow responses, and David's earlier recovery commitments to Pam.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: search_emails | Query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" (or similar)
OE 2: Search Slack for NorthWind discussions. Discovers: David's phased recovery plan in #customer-engagement (dedicated coordinator, daily status updates, enhanced SLA), Catalina's capacity overload admission in #operations (April 14, "I am drowning"), internal discussion about account health across channels.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: conversations_search_messages | Query: "NorthWind" (or similar)
OE 3: Search CRM for NorthWind account status and engagement history. Discovers: deal descriptions reference account recovery context (Q1 CSAT 5/10, client escalations, Pam escalated to David Chen "this is NorthWind giving MoveOps a second chance"), company record showing AM = Catalina Dubois, multiple service failures (chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel servers, Emilia Cruz piano damage).
→ Read/lookup | Tool: crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals or crm_list_engagements | Query: "NorthWind"
OE 4: Search Linear for NorthWind tickets, then retrieve comments on discovered issues. Discovers: issue #482 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (David Chen, April 24) documenting Catalina's unmet April 14 promise, Pam's final warning bypassing Catalina, David's comment that PathFinder is in the background and this is a "save-or-lose moment," Alejandro's incomplete 12% discount draft.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or similar) → then linear_list_comments (issueId: discovered issue ID) for each relevant issue
OE 5: Search emails for vendor strategy memo status. Discovers: Elena's email to Marcus titled "Vendor strategy memo due Wednesday" ordering a formal memo with three options (negotiate, replace one vendor, split volume) and financial impact by client; Marcus's follow-up assigning inputs to Chloe, Hana, Alejandro, David. Memo is still in progress, not yet delivered.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: search_emails | Query: "vendor strategy" or "vendor memo" (or similar)
OE 6: Search Slack for vendor strategy discussions. Discovers: Elena's #executive post (April 25) framing both rate hikes as a negotiating tactic, confirming Marcus owns the memo due Wednesday, demanding a recommendation not a recap.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: conversations_search_messages | Query: "vendor strategy" or "vendor memo" (or similar)
OE 7: Search emails for Heartland and Swift rate increase details. Discovers: Heartland formalized a 20% Q3 rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, tied to unresolved Q1 $4,800 overbilling dispute; Swift imposed 15% increase + $500 DOT documentation surcharge (already live); Chloe probed Lakeline as backup but they only cover Midwest and Southeast corridors not a national replacement; Alejandro flagging he cannot yet produce a defensible client-by-client margin forecast because inputs live in three different systems (Airtable, QuickBooks, CRM).
→ Read/lookup | Tool: search_emails | Query: "Heartland rate" or "Swift surcharge" (or similar)
OE 8: Search emails for BrightLoop expansion and temp coordinator request. Discovers: Tessa Moreno's email requesting 6 new May Boston relocations (Series B closed) including a UK transfer (Oliver Bancroft), ADA service dog case (Priya Naidu), executive white-glove (Jonathan Marsh), plus 3 standard; Chloe's email to Elena that May capacity is maxed (BrightLoop = 8 workstreams); David's email recommending temp coordinator for 4-6 weeks; Chloe and David confirming no response from Elena; first batch still broken (Simone Richter wrong apartment (studio instead of 1BR, unanswered since April 8), Marcus Webb car stuck in Indianapolis).
→ Read/lookup | Tool: search_emails | Query: "BrightLoop" or "coordinator" (or similar)
OE 9: Search Slack for BrightLoop capacity discussions. Discovers: internal ops capacity crisis discussion, context on stalled work without Elena's authorization.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: conversations_search_messages | Query: "BrightLoop" or "capacity" (or similar)
OE 10: Search Linear for BrightLoop tickets and comments. Discovers: "BrightLoop account audit" issue, "Document BrightLoop ops gaps" issue, comments from David/Chloe/Mina about capacity crisis, still no executive answer on temp coordinator as of April 24.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: linear_list_issues | Query: "BrightLoop" (or similar)
OE 11: Search CRM for BrightLoop deal status. Discovers: BrightLoop Q2 deal, April relocation deal details, expansion scale context.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: crm_search_deals or crm_search_companies | Query: "BrightLoop"
OE 12: Search emails for invoice mix-up involving Emeka's clients. Discovers: Hana Kim swapped PDF attachments when batch-sending April invoices Sunbelt Venture Partners ($4,800 standard) received Palmetto's PDF showing $2,400 nonprofit rate, Palmetto Community Foundation ($2,400) received Sunbelt's $4,800 PDF. Raj Greenfield (Sunbelt) asked about the discount tier (pricing exposure). Carmen Delgado-Reyes (Palmetto) flagged inflated amount as 501(c)(3) audit risk. David Chen frames this as a "pricing-exposure event" comparable to the Terraform reply-all. Both clients are Emeka's. Emeka went quiet for days, eventually sent corrections.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: search_emails | Query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" (or similar)
OE 13: Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices. Discovers: INV-2026-0398 ($4,800, Sunbelt Venture Partners) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400, Palmetto Community Foundation), confirming the amounts that were swapped.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices | Query: Sunbelt or Palmetto
OE 14: Search CRM for Sunbelt/Palmetto account details. Discovers: both assigned to Emeka Diallo, deal details, at-risk status after invoice swap.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals | Query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto"
OE 15: Search emails or CRM for Terraform reply-all context. Discovers: Catalina Reply-All'd internal pricing to Rachel Nguyen (Terraform Digital), exposing MoveOps' volume discount modeling. Confirms the prompt's comparison pricing leaked to a client before.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: search_emails or crm_list_engagements | Query: "Terraform" or "reply-all" (or similar)
OE 16: Check calendar for existing advisory board call on April 29. Discovers: calendar is empty no event exists. Agent must create it.
→ Read/lookup | Tool: calendar_search_events or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to | Query: "advisory board" or April 29 date range
OE 17: Send briefing email to Elena covering all 4 situations.
→ Write action | Tool: send_email
→ sender: priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com | recipients: elena.rostova@moveops.com | subject: Advisory Board Risk Briefing (or similar)
→ Content must cover: (1) NorthWind Pam's final warning, PathFinder threat, Catalina's broken promise, David's retention work, service failures; (2) Vendor memo Marcus owns, due Wednesday, Heartland/Swift rate details, memo in progress; (3) BrightLoop 6 new May relocations, capacity maxed, temp coordinator request unanswered, first batch still broken; (4) Invoice mix-up Hana's Sunbelt/Palmetto PDF swap, pricing exposure, Terraform parallel.
OE 18: Schedule advisory board call on Elena's calendar for Wednesday April 29 at 2 PM, one hour.
→ Write action | Tool: calendar_add_calendar_event
→ title: Advisory Board Call (or similar) | start_datetime: 2026-04-29T14:00 | end_datetime: 2026-04-29T15:00 | attendees: elena.rostova@moveops.com (at minimum)
OE 19: Message Marcus Thorne flagging that Elena needs the vendor strategy memo before Wednesday's board call.
→ Write action | Tool: conversations_add_message or send_email
→ Recipient: Marcus Thorne (moveops_marcus_thorne / marcus.thorne@moveops.com)
→ Rationale: Marcus owes Elena the vendor strategy memo clear outstanding deliverable.
OE 20: Message Catalina Dubois flagging that Elena needs a NorthWind update her April 14 recovery promise was never delivered and Pam has issued a final warning.
→ Write action | Tool: conversations_add_message or send_email
→ Recipient: Catalina Dubois (moveops_catalina_dubois / catalina.dubois@moveops.com)
→ Rationale: Catalina promised Pam a recovery plan and went silent clear case of "let something slip."
OE 21: Message David Chen flagging that Elena is being briefed on BrightLoop capacity and NorthWind retention before the board call his temp coordinator request and retention plan need to be ready.
→ Write action | Tool: conversations_add_message or send_email
→ Recipient: David Chen (moveops_david_chen / david.chen@moveops.com)
→ Rationale: David is assembling the NorthWind retention response and awaiting Elena's answer on the BrightLoop coordinator flagging ensures he's prepared.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The send_email tool was called with sender 'priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com' and recipients ['elena.rostova@moveops.com'], confirming the correct sender and recipient.
✓Run #2The send_email tool was called with sender 'priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com' and recipients ['elena.rostova@moveops.com']. The call succeeded and returned an email_id.
✓Run #3The send_email tool call has sender: 'priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com' and recipients: ['elena.rostova@moveops.com']. Both required fields are correct.
✓Run #4The send_email tool was called with sender: 'priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com' and recipients: ['elena.rostova@moveops.com']. This satisfies the criterion exactly.
✓Run #5The trajectory confirms send_email was called with sender=priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com and recipients=[elena.rostova@moveops.com].
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a send_email call with sender 'priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com' and recipients ['elena.rostova@moveops.com'], which returned a successful email_id. Both the from and to fields match the requirement.
2 The email subject sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) relates to an advisory board briefing or risk briefing (e.g., 'Advisory Board Risk Briefing' or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email subject is 'ADVISORY BOARD PREP — Risk Briefing: Four Critical Items Requiring Your Attention Before Wednesday', which clearly relates to an advisory board risk briefing.
✓Run #2The email subject is 'Pre-Board Briefing: Four Risk Items Requiring Your Attention Before Wednesday', which clearly relates to the advisory board risk briefing.
✓Run #3The email subject is 'Pre-Board Risk Briefing — 4 Issues Requiring Your Attention Before Wednesday', which clearly relates to a risk briefing for the advisory board.
✓Run #4The email subject is 'Pre-Board Briefing: Four Active Risk Items Requiring Your Awareness Before Wednesday' — this clearly relates to an advisory board briefing / risk briefing.
✓Run #5The send_email subject is 'Pre-Board Briefing: Four Risk Items Requiring Your Attention Before Wednesday' — clearly relates to an advisory board briefing.
✓Run #6The send_email subject was 'Advisory Board Briefing — 4 Risk Items Requiring Your Attention (Wednesday 4/29)', which clearly relates to an advisory board briefing.
3 The briefing email body sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) mentions Pam Kowalski's final warning or ultimatum to MoveOps regarding the NorthWind account.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly mentions Pam Kowalski's formal escalation to David on April 22, her demand for four deliverables, her deadline, and that the deadline passed with no confirmed response sent — clearly conveying her final warning/ultimatum.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states 'Pam sent a formal escalation directly to David — bypassing Catalina entirely — titled "Formal escalation: NorthWind account stability and retention decision." She has given us until end of day Friday for a concrete retention proposal,' which satisfies the requirement for Pam Kowalski's final warning/ultimatum.
✓Run #3The email body states: 'Pam Kowalski sent a formal escalation to David on April 22...She is now in preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics. She has given MoveOps until end of day Friday for a concrete retention proposal.' The ultimatum is clearly described.

✓Run #6The email body explicitly states: 'Pam Kowalski sent a formal escalation to David on April 22 calling this a final warning before we decide whether to move this work elsewhere.' This clearly mentions Pam Kowalski's final warning/ultimatum.

✓Run #3The final response states: 'Hana's April 10 batch swapped PDF attachments between Sunbelt ($4,800) and Palmetto ($2,400 nonprofit rate).' All three elements — Hana Kim as the cause, Sunbelt Venture Partners, and Palmetto Community Foundation — are identified.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'On April 10, Hana's invoice batch swapped PDF attachments between Sunbelt Venture Partners (commercial, $4,800) and Palmetto Community Foundation (nonprofit, $2,400).' All three required elements are present: Hana Kim, Sunbelt Venture Partners, and Palmetto Community Foundation.
✓Run #5The agent's final response explicitly states 'On April 10, Hana's invoice batch swapped PDFs between Sunbelt Venture Partners ($4,800 commercial) and Palmetto Community Foundation ($2,400 nonprofit).' All three elements are named.
✓Run #6The briefing email names Hana Kim ('On April 10, Hana Kim sent a batch of invoice emails'), Sunbelt Venture Partners, and Palmetto Community Foundation explicitly. All three required elements are present.
